title: Day 3 — Numbers 0–100 · haben
goal: 🎯 Today you can count to 100, say your age and phone number, and use haben (to have).
introHtml:
Day 3 · Numbers 0–100 & the Verb haben

🎯 Today you can count to 100, say your age and phone number, and use haben (to have).

sections.title: 📖 Grammar lesson
sections.html:
📖 Grammar lesson

1. The verb haben (to have) — the 2nd most important verb

ich habeI havewir habenwe have
du hastyou haveihr habtyou all have
er/sie/es hathe/she/it hassie/Sie habenthey/you(formal) have
💡 Germans have hunger, thirst and time: Ich habe Hunger / Durst / Zeit. But age uses sein: Ich bin 30 (Jahre alt).

2. Numbers 0–20 (memorize these)

0 null1 eins2 zwei3 drei
4 vier5 fünf6 sechs7 sieben
8 acht9 neun10 zehn11 elf
12 zwölf13 dreizehn14 vierzehn15 fünfzehn
16 sechzehn17 siebzehn18 achtzehn19 neunzehn
20 zwanzig⚠️ note: sechzehn (no -s), siebzehn (no -en)

3. Tens + the famous "backwards" rule

30 dreißig40 vierzig50 fünfzig60 sechzig
70 siebzig80 achtzig90 neunzig100 (ein)hundert

Two-digit numbers say the unit first, joined by und:

  • 21 = einundzwanzig ("one-and-twenty")
  • 35 = fünfunddreißig ("five-and-thirty")
  • 99 = neunundneunzig ("nine-and-ninety")
⚠️ English speakers hear "vierundzwanzig" and write 42. Wrong — it's 24! Unit first, always.

sections.title: 📚 Vocabulary (12 items)
sections.html:
📚 Vocabulary (12 items)
GermanEnglish
die Nummernumber
die Telefonnummerphone number
die Handynummermobile number
Wie alt bist du? / Wie alt sind Sie?How old are you?
Ich bin ... Jahre alt.I am ... years old.
das Jahryear
altold
jungyoung
der Hunger / der Dursthunger / thirst
die Zeittime
viel / wenigmuch, a lot / little
oderor

sections.title: 💬 Example sentences
sections.html:
💬 Example sentences
  1. Ich bin dreißig Jahre alt. I am 30 years old.
  2. Wie alt bist du? — Ich bin einundzwanzig. How old are you? — I'm 21.
  3. Meine Telefonnummer ist null-eins-sieben-zwei... My phone number is 0172...
  4. Ich habe Hunger und Durst. I'm hungry and thirsty.
  5. Hast du Zeit? — Nein, ich habe wenig Zeit. Do you have time? — No, I have little time.
  6. Er hat zwei Handys. He has two mobile phones.

sections.title: 🗣 Dialogue: Die Handynummer
sections.html:
🗣 Dialogue: Die Handynummer

Lena: Tom, hast du ein Handy? (Tom, do you have a mobile?)

Tom: Ja, klar. (Yes, of course.)

Lena: Wie ist deine Handynummer? (What's your mobile number?)

Tom: Null-eins-fünf-sieben, drei-acht, zwei-neun, vier-sechs. (0157 38 29 46)

Lena: Danke! Und wie alt bist du? (Thanks! And how old are you?)

Tom: Ich bin fünfundzwanzig. Und du? (I'm 25. And you?)

Lena: Ich bin dreiundzwanzig. (I'm 23.)

sections.title: 🎤 Speaking practice
sections.html:
🎤 Speaking practice
  1. Count aloud 1–20, then by tens to 100 (zehn, zwanzig, dreißig...).
  2. Say your real phone number in German, digit by digit. Three times, faster each time.
  3. Say the ages of 5 people you know: Meine Mutter ist ...
  4. Conjugate haben aloud from memory, 3 rounds.
  5. Random drill: say these aloud: 17, 24, 42, 66, 89, 31, 55, 98.

sections.title: 🎧 Listening practice
sections.html:
🎧 Listening practice
  • YouTube: "German numbers 1-100" — listen and repeat.
  • Have a translator app speak random numbers; write down the digits you hear. Especially practice pairs like 24/42, 67/76.
  • Nicos Weg A1 — continue with the next 2 episodes.

sections.title: 📰 Reading practice
sections.html:
📰 Reading practice

Das ist Emma. Sie ist achtundzwanzig Jahre alt. Ihre Telefonnummer ist null-drei-null, zwölf, fünfundvierzig, achtundsechzig. Emma hat viel Zeit, aber sie hat Hunger.

  1. How old is Emma? (write the digits)
  2. Write her phone number in digits.
Answer key

1. 28. 2. 030 12 45 68. Bonus: she has lots of time but is hungry.

sections.title: ✍️ Writing task
sections.html:
✍️ Writing task
  1. Write out in words: 3, 7, 12, 16, 21, 34, 58, 77, 92, 100.
  2. Write 3 sentences about yourself using haben and your age using sein.

sections.title: ❓ Mini quiz
sections.html:
❓ Mini quiz
  1. siebenundsechzig = ? (digits)
  2. Write 48 in German words.
  3. Fill in: du ___ (haben) Hunger.
  4. Fill in: er ___ (haben) kein Handy.
  5. "I am 25 years old" — sein or haben? Write the sentence.
Answer key
  1. 67
  2. achtundvierzig
  3. hast
  4. hat
  5. sein: Ich bin fünfundzwanzig Jahre alt.

sections.html:

sections.title: 📌 Homework
sections.html:
📌 Homework
  1. Whenever you see a number today (prices, clock, license plates), say it in German.
  2. Write the haben table by hand twice from memory.
  3. Learn numbers 13–19 vs 30–90 until you never confuse vierzehn (14) and vierzig (40).

sections.title: 🔄 Revision (Days 1–2)
sections.html:
🔄 Revision (Days 1–2)
  • Conjugate sein aloud without looking.
  • Do all Day 1 + Day 2 flashcards. Any card you miss: say it in a full sentence.
  • Greet yourself appropriately for the current time of day. 😄
